data.narrative:
Dawn Aerospace, a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $30M+, was valued using two applicable triangulation methods against a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network.

Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $30M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $57M. Peer-median analysis drew from three same-sector, same-stage private comparables: Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M), yielding a median valuation of $1.3B with a range of $400M–$2.0B. Public-comp anchoring using the Space & Aerospace sector P/S median of 64.2x (6 tickers) could not be applied due to the absence of Dawn Aerospace's revenue figures on file.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $48M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $678M. This wide range reflects material uncertainty around whether the company's trajectory and market position align with lower-funded peers or with better-capitalized players like Aalyria. Key uncertainties include revenue visibility, technical progress on core systems, and customer acquisition trajectory relative to the peer set. The stage-progression method alone yields the most conservative anchor; peer multiples introduce significant upside optionality pending further market validation.
